frameworks.name: differential correlations / information-limiting noise correlations
frameworks.one_line: Pairwise noise structure aligned with stimulus tuning derivative bounds linear Fisher information.

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Motivates whether early visual cortex preserves information matching behavioral discrimination.

frameworks.name: leaky integrate-and-fire LGN-V1 circuit model
frameworks.one_line: Spiking simulation used to validate the eigenvector-alignment detection method.

core_question: Can differential (information-limiting) correlations be reliably detected in early visual cortex with realistic neuron and trial counts?
key_assumptions: Linear Fisher information is the relevant readout bound for downstream circuits
f′ direction and noise covariance Σ can be estimated from ~100s of neurons and trials
Strong differential correlations imply f′ lies within the top eigenvectors of Σ
manipulations: Compared f′ alignment with leading vs trailing eigenvectors of Σ
Tested method on LIF LGN-V1 simulations with controlled correlation strength
Applied method to V1 population recordings in awake monkeys
key_findings: Proposed eigenvector-alignment method detects differential correlations with on the order of 100s of neurons and trials
Simulations recover correlations consistent with 0.5–3 degree orientation discrimination thresholds
V1 recordings in awake monkeys show signatures of differential correlations
interpretations: Early visual areas carry strong information-limiting correlations, constraining how much sensory information downstream circuits can read out
